prose:
Fukui reactivity indices for formaldehyde were computed using DFT, tier standard (B3LYP/def2-TZVP with density fitting) in PySCF, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. The calculation used a single-point evaluation on the input geometry (4 atoms: O, C, H, H) and derived Fukui indices from Mulliken charges of the parent neutral, cation (+1, doublet), and anion (−1, doublet) species. All SCF evaluations converged. The most electrophilic site is the carbon (f⁺ = 0.70839), and the most nucleophilic site is the oxygen (f⁻ = 0.46742). Charge conservation checks for both f⁺ and f⁻ passed (sums = 1.0000 each), confirming proper convergence of the N±1 states. The dual descriptor (Δf) reveals carbon as electrophilic (+0.5959) and the hydrogens as nucleophilic (−0.2983 each), consistent with formaldehyde's role as an electrophile in organic reactions. No warnings were reported; integrity status is trustworthy (ok).</prose>
